summary_text: Root-cause hypothesis: this regression is an ordering bug in `_post_due_jobs`. The new `_quarantine_invalid_approved_posts()` runs before duplicate arbitration, and it runs on `approved` rows in a small lateness window (`scheduled_for <= now + MAX_LATENESS_MINUTES`, currently 30). In `DuplicateDefenseTests.test_post_due_jobs_blocks_merge_duplicates_before_due_selection`, `keep` (the earliest duplicate) is in that window at `now=09:30` (`keep=10:00`), so if `can_approve(keep)` is false, `keep` is demoted to `failed` before `_check_queue_internal_duplicates()`, which then no longer treats it as an approved keeper. That changes the expected duplicate outcome. Safest fix shape: keep duplicate arbitration first, then quarantine invalid approved posts only on the post candidate path. Concretely: move `_check_queue_internal_duplicates()` to run before `_quarantine_invalid_approved_posts()` (or at least exclude canonical duplicate-keeper candidates from quarantine until after duplicate resolution). That preserves the intended “earliest approved duplicate remains keep” behavior while still running quarantine checks before posting/preflight.
